clinical_rationale: 58M POD4 post sigmoid resection for Hinchey II diverticulitis. Reports low-grade fever (38.1°C at home), wound redness at port site, and reduced mobility compared to yesterday. Pain 3/10 at wound site. Bowel function has returned (passing flatus). All other parameters within expected range for POD4. Mild deviation from expected trajectory — warrants increased vigilance and GP review within 24 hours to rule out superficial wound infection.
patient_message: Thank you for completing your check-in. Your recovery is mostly on track, but we've noticed a low-grade temperature and some redness around your wound site that need attention. Please call your GP or our nurse helpline today to arrange a wound check. If your temperature rises above 38.5°C, you develop severe pain, or your wound starts to weep, please go to your nearest Emergency Department immediately.

sbar.situation: AMBER: 58M POD4 post sigmoid resection — low-grade fever, wound erythema, reduced mobility. Risk score 0.54. Copilot recommends clinical review within 24 hours.
sbar.background: 58-year-old male underwent laparoscopic sigmoid resection for Hinchey II diverticulitis. Discharged POD2 with written wound care instructions. Check-in via app POD4. No documented allergies.
sbar.assessment: Self-reported Temp 38.1°C, Pain 3/10, wound erythema at left port site (no discharge reported). Bowel function returned — passing flatus. Mobility reduced to <100m. No signs of anastomotic leak (no peritonism, no PR bleeding reported). Risk score 0.54 (AMBER threshold).
sbar.recommendation: Telephone review by surgical nurse today. Arrange wound check — consider community nurse or GP same-day. Advise patient to take paracetamol, continue wound care, and return immediately if temperature >38.5°C, wound discharge, severe abdominal pain, or inability to eat. Follow-up clinic appointment maintained at 2 weeks.
